rationale: China's arrest of a US researcher on espionage charges is a fresh bilateral tension point; Iran deal contradiction by Tehran continues as a domestic credibility story; both sustain elevated but not escalating baselines.

top_events.title: China arrests US researcher for alleged espionage
top_events.summary: China's detention of a US academic on spying charges at a conference is a fresh escalation in US-China bilateral tensions, directly affecting Americans working or traveling in China and broader diplomatic relations.

top_events.title: Tehran says 'nothing' finalised after Trump claims Iran deal near
top_events.summary: Tehran's public contradiction of Trump's deal claims is a domestic credibility story and keeps Middle East policy uncertainty alive, with potential oil market implications for US consumers.

top_events.title: Toronto police officer shot while investigating US consulate attack
top_events.summary: An attack on the US consulate in Toronto and the shooting of a Canadian officer investigating it is a direct security incident involving US diplomatic premises on North American soil.
